summary: Risk factors for suicidal behavior do indeed differ based on affective disorder polarity due to distinct symptom presentations such as mood lability versus psychotic symptoms associated with depressive and manic episodes respectively. These variations influence how suicide risk manifests differently depending upon whether an individual has a unipolar or bipolar presentation of affective illness.
